concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes connected by edges.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: An individual element within a graph.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two nodes in a graph.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have associated numerical values.

concepts.name: Edge Weight
concepts.short_definition: The numerical value assigned to an edge.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of connected edges from one node to another.

concepts.name: Path Length
concepts.short_definition: The sum of edge weights along a path.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path with the minimum total edge weight between two nodes.

concepts.name: Single Source Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding the shortest paths from a single starting node to all other nodes.

concepts.name: Dijkstra Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the shortest paths from a single source node in a graph with non-negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Non-negative Weights
concepts.short_definition: Edge weights that are zero or positive.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that makes the locally optimal choice at each step with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Distance Array
concepts.short_definition: An array storing the shortest distance found so far from the source to each node.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A set of nodes for which the shortest path from the source has been finalized.

concepts.name: Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores elements with priorities and allows efficient retrieval of the element with the highest (or lowest) priority.

concepts.name: Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: The process of updating the shortest distance to a node if a shorter path is found through an adjacent node.

concepts.name: Initialization
concepts.short_definition: Setting initial values for distances and visited status before algorithm execution.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A single pass or step within the Dijkstra algorithm's execution.

concepts.name: Extract Min
concepts.short_definition: Operation to remove and return the node with the smallest distance from the priority queue.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each node has a list of its adjacent nodes and edge weights.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate the presence and weight of edges.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the execution time of an algorithm grows with the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the memory usage of an algorithm grows with the input size.

concepts.name: Dijkstra with Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: Dijkstra's algorithm implemented using a priority queue for efficient node selection.

concepts.name: Dijkstra without Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: Dijkstra's algorithm implemented by linearly scanning for the minimum distance node.

concepts.name: Negative Cycles
concepts.short_definition: A cycle in a graph where the sum of edge weights is negative.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path that starts and ends at the same node.

concepts.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that finds shortest paths from a single source and can handle negative edge weights and detect negative cycles.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a graph systematically.

concepts.name: Source Node
concepts.short_definition: The node from which shortest paths are calculated.

concepts.name: Destination Node
concepts.short_definition: A node for which a shortest path is being sought.

concepts.name: Path Reconstruction
concepts.short_definition: The process of determining the actual sequence of nodes forming a shortest path.

concepts.name: Predecessor Array
concepts.short_definition: An array storing the node that precedes a given node on the shortest path from the source.

concepts.name: Sparse Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph with relatively few edges compared to the number of possible edges.

concepts.name: Dense Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph with a number of edges close to the maximum possible.

concepts.name: Application of Dijkstra
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios where Dijkstra's algorithm is used.

concepts.name: Shortest Path in Maps
concepts.short_definition: Using Dijkstra to find the shortest routes between locations.

concepts.name: Network Routing
concepts.short_definition: Determining the most efficient path for data packets in computer networks.
